'''Oden Malksch''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] serving the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] before the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and the dissolution of the Jedi.

==Biography==
Trained in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Oden Malksch had risen to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] after passing his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]]. Studying along the path of the [[Jedi Consular]], Malksch specialized as a [[Lore Keeper]], becoming a noted [[Jedi historian|historian]] within the Order. Assigned to the experimental [[Almas Academy|academy]] on [[Almas]], Jedi Malksch wrote a number of papers concerning the happenings in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin System]]. Of particular interest to Malksch were the radical changes in the [[Metatheran Cartel]], the government on Almas, and the growing darkness from an ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Almas Sith fortress|fortress]] which seemed to be slowly gathering power. Malksch hoped that his writings would provide future historians an idea of the times he lived in and could use them to understand the course that history took.<ref name="AMA" />

Around [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="20 BBY">The ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' states that the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] campaign is set one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], an event that is dated to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. As the ''Living Force'' adventure ''[[Below the Belt: Philology|Philology]]'' [[Cularin's disappearance|moved]] the campaign forward ten years to the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and the adventure ''[[Night Eyes: Night's Homecoming|Night's Homecoming]]'' established that by Year 4 of the campaign over a year had elapsed since the events of ''Philology'', ''[[A Mon Alone]]'' can therefore be dated to circa [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].</ref> Malksch was appointed as the head of the Academy Archives. During his tenure there, seven bodies were found at the Sith fortress and brought to the Academy for study. Amongst the bodies were two Jedi from the academy and the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Karae Nalvas]]. After a thorough examination, Malksch discovered that their Force-essences had been uprooted from their bodies and were being stored elsewhere. Concluding that the orange jewel pendants draped around the bodies' necks were some how connected to their strange condition, Malksch broke the pendant around Nalvas' neck and found his essence transferred into his body. Conscious in the form of Dark Jedi Nalvas, Jedi Malksch could sense where the man's essence was trapped and that his body yearned to be reunited with it. Guiding a Jedi rescue team to this location, the Jedi found the trapped Force-essences of the seven bodies and all were returned to their natural states.<ref name="AMA" />
